text: In headless mode (`claude -p`), a conductor driver can launch the generated Workflow as a BACKGROUND task and then end its turn ('the run will notify me when it completes') — but ending the turn exits the -p process, killing the orphaned workflow. Observed live 2026-07-14 in an arena cell (uglify-corner-cases, opus-4-8[1m]/high): flawless Setup (DoD frozen, verify-reports.cjs grounded, per-report workers planned), then Workflow launched backgrounded, driver ended turn at ~17.5 min, process exited, wf/ branch left with ZERO commits, cell scored failed. Reproduced 2/2 on the same cell (attempts 1029s/1050s, both wf/ branches zero-commit); the same day five SHORT cells completed fine — the driver backgrounds the workflow precisely when it anticipates a LONG run, so the bug bites hardest on the hardest tasks. SKILL.md should pin the invocation: run the Workflow with run_in_background: false (or explicitly forbid ending the turn while the workflow runs — poll/wait until Finalize completes) whenever the session is non-interactive. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): SKILL.md (or the scaffold's driver instructions) contains an explicit run_in_background/do-not-end-turn directive for the Workflow invocation; today it says nothing, leaving the driver free to orphan the run.

Conductor loses to a RAW single session on wide, decomposable tasks because its generated plan runs independent work items as SEQUENTIAL phases. Measured head-to-head 2026-07-14 on arena task uglify-corner-cases (six INDEPENDENT compiler bugs, cellCapSec=3600): raw sonnet-5 one-shot RESOLVED 6/6 in ~53 min; opus-4-8[1m]/high + conductor TIMED OUT at 3603s with only 2/6 fixed — its driver designed Setup -> DoDBaseline -> Report1..Report6 -> DoDGate and paid worker spin-up + a ~5-min full-suite verification per phase, serially. The six reports were embarrassingly parallel (disjoint transforms, one shared file but separable hunks; a per-worker worktree already exists). SKILL.md / the scaffold should instruct: when the frozen DoD decomposes into independent criteria, fan the workers out concurrently (Workflow parallel()/pipeline over per-criterion workers, verify once at the gate), reserving sequential phases for genuinely dependent steps. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): generate a plan for a task whose DoD has N independent checkable criteria and assert the emitted loop runs their workers inside one parallel()/pipeline stage rather than N sequential phase() blocks; today the scaffold emits strictly sequential phases.

The --independent parallel fan-out (added by whetstone run conductor-20260714-182449) is unusable for the most common real shape of independent work: N independent changes that touch the SAME FILE. Proven live 2026-07-15 (true-v2 head-to-head, improved skill verifiably loaded via --plugin-dir): the driver read the new guidance, then correctly declined the flag — 'all six fixes touch lib/compress.js, so parallel editing would clobber' — because parallel workers share ONE worktree. SKILL.md's guidance ('no shared state') excludes shared-file tasks, so conductor stays sequential exactly where parallelism matters most (arena measured: sequential conductor times out at 3600s where a raw session resolves in ~53min). Fix: give --independent per-worker isolation — each parallel worker gets its OWN worktree/branch off the base, and a merge step (sequential rebase/cherry-pick with conflict-fix loop, or diff-level merge) combines them before the gates verify the combined diff once. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): generate with --independent and assert the emitted parallel workers are each instructed to work in a DISTINCT worktree (not the shared one) and that a merge/integrate step exists between the parallel Work phase and the gate; today all parallel workers share the single worktree and no merge step is emitted.

Even with SKILL.md pinning run_in_background:false + do-not-end-turn (kept fix from run conductor-20260714-182449, verifiably loaded), a headless opus driver STILL ended its run with the workflow unfinished: final message said 'I'm holding my turn open until the workflow finishes... Waiting for completion' and the -p process then exited at ~25min with only the setup harness committed (observed 2026-07-15, true-v2 cell). Saying it does not make it so: the driver must not emit a final text message at all while the workflow runs — the reliable pattern in -p mode is to invoke the Workflow tool with run_in_background:false so the TOOL CALL ITSELF blocks until completion, and SKILL.md should say exactly that (the tool call blocks; do not launch backgrounded and narrate waiting). Consider also instructing the driver to verify workflow status from .workflows/state/<name>.json AFTER the Workflow tool returns and before finalizing. Discriminating check is behavioral (claude -p A/B per the repo's proof norm): a headless run on a multi-phase task must end with the workflow state file status=complete/failed and a non-empty wf/ branch, not status=running; as a cheap structural proxy (RED now): SKILL.md's launch section must state that the foreground Workflow call blocks and that the driver must not end its turn with narration while status=running.
